content.problemStatement:
You are given a sorted array containing NNN integers and a number targettargettarget. Complete the given function to find the number of occurrences of target in the given array.

**Note: You need to solve this problem in O(log(n)) complexity.**


Input Format

The first line contains an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 10^5), the size of the array.
The second line contains `n` space-separated integers representing the sorted array elements.
The third line contains an integer `target` for which the occurrences need to be found.


Output Format

Return the number of occurrences of the target value in the array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines: first line contains integer N, the size of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order. Third line contains the integer target.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of occurrences of target in the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The array contains three occurrences of 5, so the answer is 3.
One test case, target 2 appears exactly once.
All ten elements are 1, therefore the count is 10.

solution.approach: Because the array is sorted we can locate the first and the last position of the target using binary search. The first position is the smallest index i such that arr[i] >= target; the last position is the largest index j such that arr[j] <= target. If the target does not exist, the first index will point to a value different from target. The answer is (last‑first+1). Both searches run in O(log N) time, giving the required complexity.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) per test case, O(T log N) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.
